meta.ui_subtitle: Theory exam · rivers, canals and lakes
meta.ui_demo: Question bank in progress. Questions derived from freely-reusable French law (the inland-navigation police regulation, the 28 Sept. 2007 order). Not an official exam; the bank keeps growing.
meta.ui_sourcenote: Source: French law (official act, freely reusable under the Open Licence / Etalab) — RIPAM, RGP, the 28 September 2007 order, Division 240. No question taken from an operator's bank.

questions.stem: A vessel's identification mark (registration) must be:

choices.text: Displayed visibly and legibly on the hull
Kept only in the boat's papers

provenance.source: Règlement général de police de la navigation intérieure (RGP/RGPNI) — Code des transports, art. R. 4241-1 et s.
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: It must be displayed visibly and legibly on the hull, so the vessel can be identified.

questions.stem: Before getting under way, what must the skipper check (duty of care)?

choices.text: The boat's condition, the safety gear and the weather
Nothing — checks are the lock's job

provenance.ref: RGP — devoir de vigilance avant le départ
provenance.source: Règlement général de police de la navigation intérieure (RGP/RGPNI) — Code des transports, art. R. 4241-1 et s.
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Ensure the boat and engine are sound, the safety equipment is aboard, and the conditions (water level, weather) are suitable.

questions.stem: On inland waters, discharging hydrocarbons, waste water or rubbish into the waterway is:

provenance.ref: RGP — rejets dans la voie d'eau
provenance.source: Règlement général de police de la navigation intérieure (RGP/RGPNI) — Code des transports, art. R. 4241-1 et s.
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Prohibited: such discharges pollute the waterway and must be collected and disposed of in the proper facilities.

questions.stem: To limit bank erosion and disturbance to wildlife, the boater should above all:

choices.text: Reduce speed and wash near the banks
Run at full speed in the centre

provenance.ref: RGP — protection des berges et de la faune
provenance.source: Règlement général de police de la navigation intérieure (RGP/RGPNI) — Code des transports, art. R. 4241-1 et s.
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Reduce speed and wash when approaching banks, reed beds and sensitive natural areas.
